name: Dorsey, Zenette
resolved_employer: Remarcable (construction-procurement software); previously Manager, Strategic Product Consulting at Procore Technologies
evidence: Zenette Dorsey is an Austin construction-software professional and Democratic civic activist. Austin Monitor (2019) places her in Austin City Council District 5 (which covers much of zip 78745) working as a strategic product consultant at Procore Technologies; her filed occupation 'Consultant' at Remarcable, another construction-procurement software firm, is a direct career continuation. The Monitor also reports she was 'an active presence in Dallas-area Democratic and political organizations prior to relocating to Austin,' consistent with the 37 FEC donations and 1.0 Democratic partisan lean in this record.

affiliations.org: The ATXelerator / Center for Austin's Future
affiliations.snippet: Dorsey is a graduate of the Center for Austin's Future's first ATXelerator class, one of 25 potential future elected officials or activists the group is cultivating; she 'has made inquiries into serving on various city boards and commissions' and hopes to run for office.

name: Buckner, Kam
resolved_employer: OUTFRONT Media — Vice President of Governmental Affairs (since 2015); concurrently Illinois State Representative, 26th District (Speaker pro tempore)
evidence: Corroborated as Kambium Elijah 'Kam' Buckner, Democratic member of the Illinois House from the 26th District. Three independent points match the filing: the reported employer/title (fields swapped) matches his documented role as VP of governmental affairs at OUTFRONT Media since 2015, and zip 60653 is Bronzeville, a neighborhood explicitly within his 26th District. He is a public official, not a private individual; only his publicly documented roles are recorded here. No Texas lobbyist registration found (he is an Illinois official); no federal PAC contributions surfaced — his FEC-adjacent footprint is as a recipient of state campaign funds, not an ideological PAC donor.

affiliations.role: State Representative; Speaker pro tempore (since Jan 2025); past chair, Illinois House Black Caucus; former Assistant Majority Leader
affiliations.snippet: Appointed to the Illinois House January 18, 2019; elected Illinois House Black Caucus chairman in December 2020; assumed the office of Speaker pro tempore on January 10, 2025.
Vice president of governmental affairs for Outfront Media (an outdoor-advertising company) since 2015; previously government relations director for the Chicago Cubs and an aide to Sen. Dick Durbin.

affiliations.org: Illinois assault weapons ban (Protect Illinois Communities Act) — legislative support
affiliations.role: Supporting state legislator / public advocate for the ban
affiliations.snippet: On his official legislative site, Rep. Buckner states he was proud to help enshrine Illinois' assault weapons ban into law and responded in support of the Seventh Circuit decision upholding it. (Chief sponsor of the bill was Rep. Bob Morgan, not Buckner.)
Board of Trustees member for Chicago State University, 2017-2023; also an instructor at the University of Chicago.

name: Smith, Gregory
evidence: Gregory L. Smith is President & CEO of the Austin Revitalization Authority, the East Austin 501(c)(3) community development corporation that served as lead developer for the East 11th and 12th Street Redevelopment Program; his zip 78724 is East Austin, consistent with the organization's service area. ProPublica's IRS Form 990 data independently lists 'Gregory Smith, President' with FY2024 compensation of $213,622 on ~$1.06M total revenue. A lifelong Austinite, he previously spent roughly 18 years managing community development programs for the City of Austin's Neighborhood Housing and Community Development Department. Note: although classified Nonprofit / Advocacy, ARA's core activity is real-estate redevelopment in a corridor subject to active City land-use decisions, which is relevant context for a District 1 council donation.

affiliations.snippet: IRS Form 990 data lists Gregory Smith as President of the Austin Revitalization Authority, a 501(c)(3) classified under 'Community Improvement, Capacity Building / Urban, Community Economic Development,' with FY2024 compensation of $213,622 plus $32,988 other benefits.

affiliations.role: Lead developer (via ARA); previously implemented the program as a City of Austin staffer
affiliations.snippet: When the city adopted the redevelopment plan in 1999 it signed an agreement with the Urban Renewal Board and the Austin Revitalization Authority, which served as lead developer; the ARA-city partnership was dissolved in 2010 and ARA now operates as a private nonprofit.

name: Samson, Joi
resolved_employer: Pediatrix Neonatology of Tennessee (Pediatrix Medical Group) — neonatologist
evidence: Resolved to Dr. Autumn Joi Goodwin-Samson, M.D., board-certified in Pediatrics and Neonatal-Perinatal Medicine, practicing with Pediatrix Neonatology of Tennessee at Methodist Le Bonheur Germantown Hospital. Her practice address, 7691 Poplar Ave, Germantown TN, carries zip 38138 — an exact match to the filing's zip — and the filed 'Doctor' / 'Pedatrix' fields match her specialty and employer. Howard University College of Medicine (2004), pediatrics residency at UT Health Science Center (2007), neonatal-perinatal fellowship at UT Health Science Center (2010). Employer-fallback classification: all three mandatory searches were run and returned no federal PAC giving, no Texas lobbyist registration, and no board seats or policy-advocacy roles on her full professional profile.

Public-records aggregators place a Kathy/Katherine Pavlovich in Leander 78641, and CorporationWiki lists a Kathy Pavlovich as a member of Ezell-Pavlovich Marketing LLC (Austin) — same name and same metro, but nothing in the public record ties that LLC to this donor's stated 'Contractor' occupation, so the classification rests on the self-employed occupation in the filing itself. No news coverage, bio page, board seat, or professional profile found. Employer-fallback classification only.

name: Hamilton III, Edward
resolved_employer: Amwins (specialty/wholesale insurance brokerage), Charleston SC area — sales role per the filing
evidence: Filing lists occupation 'Sales' and employer 'Amwins' at zip 29403 (peninsular Charleston, SC); Amwins is a large wholesale/specialty insurance brokerage with Charleston-area offices, which places the donor in insurance brokerage (Finance under this taxonomy). No LinkedIn profile, firm bio, news mention, board seat, or other public profile for this specific individual was locatable — the employer/occupation on the filing is the only public identifier. The 93 federal contributions and Republican-side partisan lean noted in the input could not be independently verified: FEC's public API returned HTTP 429 rate-limit responses throughout this session, the FEC data UI is client-rendered and returned no records to a fetch, and OpenSecrets' donor-lookup returned HTTP 403. Treat the federal-giving lean as unverified here rather than as a documented finding. Employer-fallback classification only.

Former Travis County assistant county attorney and assistant district attorney; long-serving Austin city board/commission member and a well-known figure in Travis County Democratic politics (he was the front-running, later withdrawn, candidate for Travis County Democratic Party chair in 2017), consistent with the input's 0.99 Democratic partisan lean. name: Ruiz, Emmy
resolved_employer: Self-employed political consultant, Washington DC; previously White House Director of Political Strategy and Outreach and Assistant to the President (2021–Jan 2025), and co-founder/partner at NEWCO Strategies
evidence: Best match is Emmy Ruiz, the Democratic political strategist who served as White House Director of Political Strategy and Outreach under President Biden (Jan 2021 – Jan 2025, elevated to Assistant to the President in Sept 2022). Corroborating factors: uncommon name; the donor's Washington DC address (20017), consistent with her DC-based White House tenure; occupation 'Consultant' with self as employer, matching her pre- and post-administration consulting career; strongly Democratic federal giving (input lean 1.0); and prior Austin/Texas ties — she was described as an 'Austin-based political consultant' when appointed in 2021 and was raised in La Feria, TX. No public record directly links the specific 20017 record to her, so identity is corroborated by pattern rather than by a source document; treated as medium confidence. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found. Note the industry classification (Consulting / PR) holds regardless of which DC consultant this is.

affiliations.role: Director of Political Strategy and Outreach; Assistant to the President (2021–Jan 2025)
affiliations.snippet: Texas Tribune, Jan 5 2021: 'Austin-based political consultant Emmy Ruiz to join White House as director of political strategy and outreach.' She was elevated to Assistant to the President in September 2022.

In February 2019 Ruiz became a partner at NEWCO Strategies, a DC-based, majority LGBTQ+- and women-owned strategic consulting firm; she also served as senior advisor to Kamala Harris's 2020 primary campaign and ran Clinton 2016 state operations in NV, OH and CO.
